methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $169M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.5B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies is a Series F climate and energy company that has raised $169M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Helion (Series F, $5.4B valuation), TAE Technologies (Series G, $6.0B), and Form Energy (Series G, $3.5B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $169M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement, implying a $220M valuation. Peer-median analysis leverages the three comparable companies, which span a $3.5B–$6.0B range with a median of $5.4B. This substantial gap reflects the wide dispersion in private frontier-tech valuations at comparable development stages.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $187M (low) to $6.2B (high), with a median of $2.8B. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed; the peer-median method reflects recent market valuations for functionally similar-stage competitors but exhibits material uncertainty given the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the climate-tech sector. Key downside scenarios include prolonged commercialization timelines and subsidy policy shifts; upside depends on accelerated technology deployment and customer adoption relative to peer benchmarks.
